question: Continue DinoBrain OS completion hardening by implementing the next missing hard verifier for dynamic live semantic retrieval and answer-quality readiness.

items.title: Configured hooks are not live preflight proof
items.summary: A configured or probe-verified DinoBrain hook must not be claimed as fully live until a fresh trusted Codex or Claude prompt produces pre-response events before manual MCP calls. | Rule: Distinguish configured, probe-verified, and live-verified; require fresh prompt event evidence for live hook claims. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: RAG quality requires hybrid retrieval, provenance, and evaluation
items.summary: DinoBrain retrieval should evolve beyond lexical search toward contextual chunks, BM25 plus dense hybrid retrieval, rank fusion, reranking, durable source provenance, canaries, and behavior evaluation. | Rule: When improving DinoBrain search, separate anchor-only sources from verified chunks, keep retrieval mode honest, and evaluate memory-on behavior against baselines. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: DinoBrain must run pre-response and fail closed
items.summary: The user wants every Codex or Claude session to receive DinoBrain pre-response OS context before substantive work, with fail-closed behavior when the OS context is missing. | Rule: For nontrivial DinoBrain work, require os_begin_task or trusted hook preflight, Context Pack, gates, finish_task, and explicit degraded/fail-closed state when missing. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: Observability must show real OS state
items.summary: The user wants Observatory and logs to show real hook/preflight/task/context/audit/trust/graph/sync states, including blocked, pending, degraded, verifier, and active work. | Rule: Do not treat a decorative graph as sufficient; expose live OS traces, graph health, gate status, audit trust, memory use, sync risk, and stale task state. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: Version drift and stale release artifacts are recurring risks
items.summary: DinoBrain app, data, installer, local checkout, release ZIP, tag, and GitHub asset can drift; version and artifact alignment must be checked before claiming install or release readiness. | Rule: For releases and installer updates, verify local/remote refs, package version, embedded installer refs, tag, ZIP/SHA asset, and GitHub release state. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: Explain unfamiliar work in plain language
items.summary: The user asks for plain explanations when a task, state, or warning is unclear, and wants the practical meaning rather than jargon. | Rule: When the user asks what something means or why it happened, explain the concrete cause, consequence, and next action in plain Korean. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: Carry authorized work through commit, push, and deploy
items.summary: When the user explicitly authorizes implementation or deployment, they expect Codex to carry the work through commit, push, release, and verification rather than stopping at a proposal. | Rule: After explicit authorization such as go, push, deploy, or auto-commit, implement, verify, commit, push, and report exact hashes or links. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: DinoBrain goal is knowledge compounding from sessions
items.summary: The core DinoBrain goal is a compounding LLM Wiki where user sessions become reviewed preferences, decisions, rules, corrections, and source-backed knowledge that improve future sessions. | Rule: Treat sessions as the root source; convert them into reviewed memory, lifecycle-clean noisy memory, evaluate behavior lift, and retrieve the result in later and future Context Packs. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review
